title: Kangaroo post-200 guard probe
summary: Two API-free post-200 guard patches preserved the stable first 200-point reward but did not exceed the plateau. Action probes show the policy enters a ladder-top state where no atomic action can escape before life loss.

context.base_result: The shaped policy_v2 reached 200 in 31 of 32 standardized follow-up episodes but never exceeded 200.

post200_guard.change: Disable monkey punch priority when the player is already on a ladder column and near the ladder top.

post200_guard_dodge.change: Use the post-200 punch guard and let coconut danger override near-top dismount.

probe_step_610.state: After first reward, player at x=132,y=140 with score 200.
probe_step_610.finding: Horizontal actions do not dismount. UP moves to y=132, while DOWN and DOWN-diagonal actions move back downward toward y=156.

probe_step_805.state: Near terminal failure, player at x=132,y=132 with falling coconut at x=133,y=121.
probe_step_805.finding: No atomic action among the 18 available actions moves the player or avoids the imminent life loss.

interpretation: The post-200 failure is not solved by simply lowering monkey punch priority.
The policy reaches a ladder-top trap: by y=132 it is too late for any action to escape.
At y=140 the controller should not continue UP into y=132, but the available immediate alternatives either freeze horizontally or move back down the ladder.
The next useful work is a staged mechanics probe to discover the correct transition at the first ladder/top boundary, not another broad LLM run.

rq_mapping.RQ1: Shows that local guards preserve the stable first reward but do not create a higher-scoring Kangaroo policy.
rq_mapping.RQ2: Strengthens the behavioral diagnosis: the plateau is a route-transition/mechanics failure, not only punch farming.
rq_mapping.RQ4: Shows that parameter/search shaping alone is insufficient unless the policy structure exposes the correct top-of-ladder transition.

recommended_next_steps: Build a small top-of-ladder transition probe that searches short action sequences from the y=140 state and scores whether the player reaches a new platform or next ladder without losing a life.
Only after identifying a working transition sequence should the policy or prompt be updated.
Do not promote any of the post-200 guard patches; they remain exploratory negative results.
